title: anacardium giganteum
intro: Anacardium giganteum, also known as the poison tree or marking nut tree, is a species of plant native to South America, belonging to the cashew family (Anacardiaceae). The plant's seeds and sap contain a resin called anacardic acid, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Anacardium giganteum include its potential to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and support immune function, although it is essential to use caution due to the toxic nature of its resin. Anacardium giganteum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or ointments, and is often used in folk medicine, but its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
